reading_plan.reading_goal: Reconstruct the research evolution trajectory of developing a new method for customizing protein expression vectors for fast, efficient, and background-free parallel cloning. Identify the initial problem, constraints, hypotheses, vector design and cloning strategy, experimental validations (in E. coli, insect cells, HEK293E cells), observed results, any failures or partial results, revisions, and limitations.

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The paper addresses the need for a fast, efficient, and background-free parallel cloning method to customize protein expression vectors for screening across multiple hosts (E. coli, insect cells, HEK293E cells). Existing parallel vector series have compromises, and methods like DpnI digestion are not 100% efficient in counterselection, leading to background colonies. The goal is to develop a method that allows any selected vector to be tailored for parallel SLIC cloning with zero background, enabling efficient screening of protein constructs, tags, and expression hosts.

section_gists.heading: A new method to customize protein expression vectors for fast, efficient and background free parallel cloning
Abstract
section_gists.gist: Summarizes the problem: need for parallel screening of protein variants, tags, hosts. Describes the solution: a fast, efficient, background-free cloning approach using SLIC with ccdB counterselection. Reports >95% cloning efficiency, >30 vectors generated, >250 genes cloned, and successful expression in E. coli, insect cells, HEK293E cells.
process_signals: Established a fast, efficient and absolutely background free cloning approach.
Modified expression vectors based on pET, pFastBac and pTT backbones.
Introduced toxic ccdB gene under strong constitutive promoter for counterselection.
ccdB is 100% efficient in killing parental vector carrying cells.
Clone efficiency is >95% with clone numbers up to 200.
All expression vectors showed efficient expression of eGFP and different target proteins.
methods: HRV 3C protease cleavage site as common primer binding site.

section_gists.heading: Background
section_gists.gist: Describes the initial problem: as a core facility, they need to screen many parameters (constructs, tags, hosts) for proper protein folding. Existing parallel cloning methods (Gateway, type II restriction enzymes, Golden Gate, RF cloning, SLIC) and commercial/non-commercial vector series have limitations. They already had established pETM, pFastBac, pTT vectors and wanted to customize them rather than adapt to a new system. Requirements: sequence-independent, directional, precise, fast, simple, efficient, cost-effective. Hypothesis: strong constitutive ccdB expression can provide 100% counterselection. Design: use HRV 3C site and 3' end of ccdB as common primer binding sites.
process_signals: Initial focus on constructs and solubility tags in E. coli.
Eukaryotic hosts used if E. coli failed; decided to implement parallel testing.
Required efficient parallel cloning method.
Existing methods had compromises; preferred to customize existing vectors.
Hypothesis: ccdB introduced for strong constitutive expression to suppress growth at 100% efficiency.
Design: HRV 3C cleavage site and 3' end of ccdB as common primer binding sites.
failures_or_limits: Existing parallel vector series have compromises.

section_gists.heading: Vector design and cloning strategy
section_gists.gist: Details the design: used OmpA promoter (llp5 variant) and Shine-Dalgarno for strong constitutive ccdB expression. pPCRScript-LPP5-ccdB and all pCoofy ccdB derivatives show 100% killing efficiency. Occasionally observed ccdB inactivation during propagation in ccdB Survival cells under high selective pressure, so killing activity must be verified for each batch. First cloned Llp5-ccdB into pETM14,22,33,44 to generate pCoofy1-4. Established parallel SLIC cloning procedure using eGFP.

Inserted Shine-Dalgarno sequence upstream of ccdB.
pPCRScript-LPP5-ccdB and pCoofy ccdB derivatives show 100% killing efficiency.

Killing activity must be verified for every single batch of vector DNA.

Established parallel SLIC cloning procedure using eGFP.

section_gists.heading: Vector list and cloning statistics
section_gists.gist: Extended pCoofy vector list by modifying additional pETM vectors or introducing new tags (His10, OneStrep, S, Halo). All N-terminal tags followed by HRV 3C site. Generated SUMO tag vectors for native N-terminus after cleavage. Also generated vectors with C-terminal His10 or OneStrep tags. Designed 2nd generation ccdB cassette including C-tags to increase variations while reducing number of vectors. Cloning statistics: >95% positive clones, average 15-31 clones per construct depending on insert size, no size restriction.

No size restriction for gene insertion.

section_gists.heading: Expression in E.coli
section_gists.gist: Validated each new vector with eGFP. Removed vectors with unexpectedly low expression. Example: His10-Trx-eGFP expressed at ~20%, but switching tag order to Trx-His10-eGFP increased expression to >70%. eGFP fusions: Trx, MBP, NusA gave highest solubility and expression (up to 80%). His6-GST-eGFP expressed high but low solubility, corroborating previous observation that His6-GST from pETM33 was insoluble. For target proteins, MBP had major impact on solubility. Pil mutants: expression 50-80%, His6-GST fusion lower/no expression; only His6-MBP-Pil soluble after tag removal.
process_signals: Validated each new vector with eGFP; removed vectors with low expression.
Tag order affected expression: His10-Trx-eGFP low, Trx-His10-eGFP high.
His6-GST-eGFP high expression but low solubility; consistent with previous pETM33 insolubility.
MBP had major impact on solubility for target proteins.
Pil mutants: His6-GST fusion lower/no expression; only His6-MBP-Pil soluble after tag removal.
methods: Small scale expression in E. coli
metrics: Expression levels up to 80% of total protein
failures_or_limits: Vectors removed due to low expression.
Some constructs not expressed or insoluble.

section_gists.heading: Expression in insect cells
section_gists.gist: Parallel screening allowed easy switch from E. coli to baculovirus. Examples: Vasp (degradation in E. coli, successful in insect cells with His6-GST and His6-MBP), ODC (needed insect cell expression for co-expression), Top1 (His6-MBP much higher expression than His6-GST).
process_signals: Parallel cloning enabled rapid host switching.
Vasp: degradation in E. coli, successful in insect cells.
ODC: properly folded in E. coli but needed insect cells for co-expression.
Top1: His6-MBP higher expression than His6-GST in insect cells.
methods: Baculovirus expression in SF9 and High Five cells

section_gists.heading: Expression in HEK293E cells
section_gists.gist: Standard IMAC purification not efficient in HEK293E cells, so introduced alternative C-terminal tags. ccdB-C-tag cassette increases plasmid size by 1350 bp; tested impact on transient expression: eGFP and CD40 ligand showed comparable levels from original pTT and pCoofy derivatives. Tested C-terminal tags: S, His10, HPC, CBP, OneStrep. Best expression for S and His10; best yield/purity for OneStrep and His10 with stringent imidazole washes.
process_signals: IMAC purification not efficient in HEK293E cells, so introduced alternative C-terminal tags.
ccdB-C-tag cassette increases plasmid size by 1350 bp; tested impact on expression.
Comparable expression levels from original pTT and pCoofy derivatives.
Best yield/purity for OneStrep and His10 tags.
failures_or_limits: Standard IMAC not efficient in HEK293E cells.
Some tags (S-tag, HPC, CBP) gave lower yield.

section_gists.heading: Conclusions
section_gists.gist: Developed method to tailor any expression vector for efficient, fast, robust, cost-effective parallel cloning. High efficiency due to 100% ccdB counterselection vs DpnI. Generated >30 parallel vectors for bacteria, insect, HEK293 cells with various tags. Method is robust and easily implemented. Limitations: need for ccdB survival strains, potential size increase of vectors, specific applications not tested.
process_signals: Method allows tailoring any expression vector for parallel cloning.
100% ccdB counterselection superior to DpnI.
Generated >30 parallel vectors.
Method is robust and easily implemented.
Limitations: need for ccdB survival strains, potential size increase, specific applications not tested.

section_gists.heading: Vector construction
section_gists.gist: Used standard molecular biology methods. ccdB survival cells for propagating ccdB plasmids, OmniMAX 2 T1R for cloning reactions. pETM vectors from EMBL, pTT from Yves Durocher, pFastBac from Life Technologies. Synthetic ccdB DNA from Sloning; 2nd generation cassette from GeneArt. pCoofy E. coli vectors: ligated Llp5-ccdB HindIII fragment into pETM. Added tags by PCR. pCoofy insect vector from pIEX1; baculovirus vectors from pFastBac1; HEK vector from pTT5. Verified integrity by sequencing; verified ccdB toxicity for each vector/prep; controlled functionality by eGFP cloning and test expression.
process_signals: Used ccdB survival cells for propagation.
Synthetic ccdB DNA from Sloning; 2nd generation from GeneArt.
Verified ccdB toxicity for each vector and preparation.
Controlled functionality by eGFP cloning and test expression.
failures_or_limits: ccdB toxicity must be verified for each batch.

section_gists.heading: SLIC cloning
section_gists.gist: Vectors PCR linearized with LP1/LP2 primers, purified, stored ready-to-use. Insert PCR with gene-specific primers plus 15-25 bp extensions complementary to LP1/LP2. Standard PCR conditions. SLIC reaction: 100 ng vector, 1:3 molar ratio insert, recA protein, 30 min 37°C, transform into OmniMAX 2 T1R cells. Typically 3 clones sequenced per construct.
process_signals: Vectors linearized by PCR and stored ready-to-use.
Insert primers have 15-25 bp extensions complementary to vector primers.
SLIC reaction uses recA protein.
Typically 3 clones sequenced per construct.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The research evolved from the need to efficiently screen multiple parameters for protein production in a core facility setting.
Initial hypothesis: strong constitutive ccdB expression can provide 100% counterselection, eliminating background colonies.
Design phase: selected OmpA promoter and Shine-Dalgarno for ccdB expression; used HRV 3C site and ccdB 3' end as common primer binding sites for SLIC.

Validation phase: tested cloning efficiency (>95%), expression in E. coli (removed low-expressing vectors, optimized tag order), insect cells (enabled host switching), HEK293E cells (addressed purification challenges, tested impact of cassette size).
Iterative refinement: extended vector series with new tags, designed 2nd generation cassette to reduce vector number, verified ccdB toxicity for each batch.
Final method: robust, cost-effective, easily implemented, but requires ccdB survival strains and may increase vector size.

evidence_bank.quote: In order to handle all these different expression constructs, an efficient parallel cloning method was required.
ccdB introduced into the vector series was designed for strong constitutive expression in order to suppress growth in non-resistant cells at 100% efficiency.
We first cloned Llp5-ccdB into pETM14, pETM22, pETM33 and pETM44 to generate pCoofy1, 2, 3 and 4
Addition of recA raised overall cloning efficiency to >95%.
We tested several other variations to the basic protocol, however none of these further improved cloning efficiency

We tested several other variations to the basic protocol, however none of these further improved cloning efficiency: number of PCR cycles, PCR without extension step, extended LP1 and LP2 primer length for vector and insert PCR amplification, amount of vector and insert, molar ratio of vector and insert, 5 min 95°C denaturation of vector and insert mix followed by slow renaturation at 22° (data not shown).
We have effectively cloned more than 250 inserts into all different vectors of the pCoofy series.
As we never observed any background, we were not concerned if clone numbers were low, as the clone was correct in almost all cases (Figure 4).

Prior to use for requested target proteins, every new vector was validated with eGFP for cloning and small scale expression in the respective host. In case the expression level was unexpectedly low, we removed the vector from the list.
In agreement with the eGFP expression data, MBP had a major impact on protein solubility (Figure 5B).
expressed both constructs in SF9 cells without any signs of degradation
Protein purification from HEK293E cells in our hands is not very efficient using standard immobilized metal affinity purification.
We therefore have introduced alternative C-terminal purification tags into pTT5
we have shown effective protein expression and purification from pCoofy40 vectors that can now be included in our parallel cloning strategy
pCoofy E.coli expression vectors were generated by ligating the Llp5-ccdB HindIII restriction enzyme fragment from pPCRSript-ccdB into HindIII linearized pETM vectors.
The pCoofy transient insect expression vector was derived from pIEX1.
The pCoofy Baculovirus expression vectors were derived from pFastBac1.

canonical_events.summary: Need for efficient parallel cloning method to screen constructs, tags, and hosts for protein production.
Hypothesis that strong constitutive ccdB expression can provide 100% counterselection.
Design of ccdB cassette with OmpA promoter (llp5 variant) and Shine-Dalgarno sequence for strong constitutive expression.
Cloning of Llp5-ccdB into pETM vectors to generate first pCoofy vectors (pCoofy1-4).
Establishment of parallel SLIC cloning procedure using eGFP with recA protein, achieving >95% cloning efficiency.
Testing variations to SLIC protocol (PCR cycles, primer length, vector/insert ratio, denaturation/renaturation) did not further improve cloning efficiency.
Cloning of more than 250 inserts into pCoofy vectors with no background and high clone correctness; average ~20 clones per reaction, insert sizes 150-3939 bp.
Validation of each new vector with eGFP; removal of vectors with unexpectedly low expression (e.g., His10-Trx-eGFP ~20% vs Trx-His10-eGFP >70%).

findings.problem: Step 9 incorrectly includes evidence EV_C003_E04_01 (HEK293E purification problem) which belongs to canonical event K14, not K13. This conflates insect cell expression with the HEK293E purification issue.
findings.revision_instruction: Remove EV_C003_E04_01 from step 9's evidence list and ensure step 9 only covers insect cell expression (K13). The HEK293E purification problem should be addressed in a separate step (e.g., step 10).

findings.problem: Step 10 is labeled as 'analysis' but describes a design/experiment action (introducing alternative C-terminal purification tags). It should be 'design' or 'experiment' to match the event type of K15.
findings.revision_instruction: Change the phase of step 10 from 'analysis' to 'design' or 'experiment' to accurately reflect the action of introducing alternative tags.

findings.problem: Step 7 mentions specific expression percentages (~20% vs >70%) for His10-Trx-eGFP and Trx-His10-eGFP, but these numbers are not directly supported by the cited evidence EV_C002_E04_01. The evidence only states that vectors with unexpectedly low expression were removed, without providing these exact figures.
findings.revision_instruction: Either add evidence that explicitly states the ~20% and >70% expression levels, or remove these specific numbers from the observation and rely on the general statement about removing low-expression vectors.

findings.problem: Step 8 mentions 'pCoofy1-4 vectors' but the evidence EV_AUTO_007 only mentions 'pCoofy1, 2, 3, and 4' without the hyphenated form. While minor, consistency in vector naming is important.
findings.revision_instruction: Use the exact vector names as in the evidence: 'pCoofy1, 2, 3, and 4' instead of 'pCoofy1-4'.

findings.problem: Step 11 includes evidence EV_C003_E04_01 (HEK293E purification problem) which is not directly related to the validation of purification tags (K17). This evidence belongs to the problem statement (K14) and should not be repeated here.
findings.revision_instruction: Remove EV_C003_E04_01 from step 11's evidence list to keep the focus on the validation of OneStrep and His10 tags.

must_keep: The overall trajectory covers all required mainline events (K01-K17 except K05, K06, K10, K16 which are not required).
The candidate correctly captures the hypothesis, design, construction, validation, and application of the pCoofy vector system.
Key numbers (e.g., >95% efficiency, >250 inserts, 50-80% expression) are accurately reported where supported.
The success verification and limitations are consistent with the paper.
self_critic.summary: The candidate is largely accurate and covers all required canonical events. However, there are a few issues: step 9 conflates insect cell expression with the HEK293E purification problem by including irrelevant evidence; step 10 is mislabeled as 'analysis' instead of 'design'/'experiment'; step 7 includes unsupported specific expression percentages; and steps 8 and 11 have minor evidence consistency issues. These should be corrected to improve precision and alignment with the event graph.

The pCoofy HEK expression vector was derived from pTT5. The vector was linearized with EcoRI / NotI and ligated to the ccdB - C - tag cassette, which was PCR amplified with EcoRI / NotI primer extensions.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Alternative C-terminal tags introduced into pTT5-based pCoofy vectors.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify the best purification tag for yield and purity in HEK293E cells.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: OneStrep and His10 tags with stringent imidazole washes will give best yield and purity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test purification of eGFP fusions with different C-terminal tags from HEK293E cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Best yield and purity were obtained for eGFP-One Strep and eGFP-His10 when washed stringently with 50mM and 80mM imidazole. We have shown effective protein expression and purification from pCoofy40 vectors.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Method ready for parallel cloning strategy across hosts.

evidence.quote_or_span: we have shown effective protein expression and purification from pCoofy40 vectors that can now be included in our parallel cloning strategy

validation_methods: eGFP expression assay for each new vector
Small-scale test expression in appropriate host
success_verification.final_conclusion: The pCoofy vector series with ccdB counterselection enables fast, efficient, and background-free parallel cloning for protein expression screening across E. coli, insect cells, and HEK293E cells. MBP tag improves solubility; OneStrep and His10 tags enable effective purification from HEK293E cells.
limitations: ccdB survival strains required for vector propagation
Vector size increase due to ccdB cassette
Standard IMAC inefficient in HEK293E cells without optimized tags
